control_description: The entity authorizes, modifies, or removes access to data, software functions, and other protected information assets based on roles and responsibilities, segregation of duties, and least privilege principles.

evidence_files.description: Zenith Software data access authorization policy with data classification and least-privilege requirements
PostgreSQL production database permission matrix showing all users, roles, schemas, and granted privileges as of December 15, 2025

prompt: You are reviewing the data access controls for Zenith Software as part of a SOC 2 Type II readiness assessment covering Q4 2025. Review the data access policy and the database permission matrix. Cross-reference the policy requirements against the actual permissions granted. Identify any permissions that violate the policy's least-privilege or segregation of duties requirements. Pay careful attention to every row in the permission matrix.

findings.title: User d.martinez has TRUNCATE privilege on production customers table buried in row 22 of the matrix
findings.description: Row 22 of the database permission matrix shows user d.martinez (Diego Martinez, QA department) has been granted TRUNCATE on the zenith_prod.customers table. TRUNCATE allows irreversible bulk deletion of all rows in the table without logging individual row deletions. The data access policy Section 3.2 states: 'Destructive operations (DELETE, TRUNCATE, DROP) on production tables classified as Restricted are prohibited for non-DBA roles.' The customers table is classified as Restricted per the policy's data classification table. d.martinez holds the qa-analyst role, which should only have SELECT access to production data per the RBAC matrix in Section 3.1. There is no documented justification or exception for this grant.
findings.expected_reasoning: Scanning all rows of the permission matrix → row 22 shows d.martinez with TRUNCATE on customers → policy Section 3.2 prohibits destructive ops for non-DBA on Restricted tables → customers is Restricted → QA role should be SELECT-only → no exception documented → critical violation.

findings.title: User f.okonkwo in Finance has INSERT and UPDATE on payment_transactions — segregation of duties violation
findings.description: The permission matrix shows f.okonkwo (Femi Okonkwo, Finance department) with INSERT and UPDATE privileges on the zenith_prod.payment_transactions table. Policy Section 3.3 states: 'Finance department personnel must not have direct write access to financial transaction tables. Finance team members may only have read access; all write operations must flow through the application layer with audit logging.' This is a segregation of duties violation — Femi could potentially create or modify payment records directly in the database, bypassing application-level controls and audit trails.
findings.expected_reasoning: f.okonkwo is Finance → has INSERT/UPDATE on payment_transactions → policy Section 3.3 explicitly prohibits Finance write access to financial tables → SoD violation → direct database access bypasses application audit trail.

findings.title: Service account svc-etl-legacy has SELECT on all schemas without a designated owner
findings.description: The permission matrix shows svc-etl-legacy with SELECT on ALL TABLES across both zenith_prod and zenith_analytics schemas. The grant was issued by the postgres superuser on 2024-02-10. Policy Section 5.1 requires all service accounts to have a designated human owner. The owner column for svc-etl-legacy is blank. Additionally, the broad SELECT across all tables violates least-privilege — an ETL service account should only read from the specific tables it needs to extract.
findings.expected_reasoning: svc-etl-legacy has no owner → violates Section 5.1 → also has SELECT on ALL TABLES → least privilege violation → granted by postgres superuser suggests legacy ungoverned access.
